title: [CI:DOCS] Cirrus: Support using updated/latest NV/AV in PRs
body:
On occasion, developers need to run the latest or bleeding-edge netavark/aardvark-dns in the podman CI environment.  Enable this through use of magic strings in the PR title, but only if the PR is marked as a draft.  The intent being, when the PR is ready for review, the current CI VM package versions will be used.  Hopefully also reminding the PR author to remove the magic strings from the title.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: @cevich The build script is failing though

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *flouthoc* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> I think this is missing the point that we want to keep the newer version for all PRs after that.

It's not safe to update the CI VM images in a podman PR since the images are used for multiple repos (i.e. buildah).  So incorporating a new/updated version would need to go through the c/automation_images build process to do what you're saying.  I realize this is less than ideal, but I can't think of another way to do it safely - only for podman CI.  We would need dedicated podman-only CI VM images instead of shared ones.  Possible to do, but a PITA to maintain.

What may help eventually is, I've been playing with using renovate to help update our CI VM images.  Such that they could be rebuilt often, like once per week, and renovate would open a PR in each repo. to run CI.  It's conceivable the build process could be automated at some point as well, but I think this is all quite a ways down the road.  The renovate setup itself seems pretty straight-forward, like something I could complete with a weeks-work or so (assuming renovate is enabled universally).

Unless I'm misunderstanding still, and/or maybe you have an idea how both stability and easy/tested updates, can be achieved?
Note: The method used in this PR results in nearly every single one of about 50 tasks, updating netavark/aardvark.  This is ripe for causing flakes if done frequently or on a large-scale.

@cevich I agree with you and also with @Luap99 , that once the PR goes green inorder to merge we will need podman CI to start using new netavark/aardvark. To me going the automation image route looks fine since it will not magically break things.

Like for example as of now I think podman's CI should start using recently released netavark/aardvark do you think we should update automation images.


> What may help eventually is, I've been playing with using renovate to help update our CI VM images. Such that they could be rebuilt often, like once per week, and renovate would open a PR in each repo. to run CI. It's conceivable the build process could be automated at some point as well, but I think this is all quite a ways down the road. The renovate setup itself seems pretty straight-forward, like something I could complete with a weeks-work or so (assuming renovate is enabled universally).
> 
> Unless I'm misunderstanding still, and/or maybe you have an idea how both stability and easy/tested updates, can be achieved?

Making renovate  do it once a week SGTM as a long term fix.

Okay good, yes I see the issue both ways as well.  I also recognize that rebuilding the CI VM images can be a daunting task.  I'm doing what I can to address the painful areas, at the same time I appreciate that the whole team benefits from CI VM images that are kept current.

Rolling out renovate is definitely going to be a longer-term prospect, and teaching it to manage CI VM images will be a further, follow-on addition to that effort.  I'm making progress, it's just slow going with keeping up on all my other tasks.  All that said, I'm fine either way if we want to just close this PR, or merge it as a short-term stop-gap, or if some other third option occurs to somebody.

> Maybe CI:NVAV=COPR and CI:NVAV=DNF? 

That's an interesting suggestion actually, probably would be easier to remember.
For extra credit, if this is possible, it'd be nice to make the check be `CI:[AN]V[AN]V=`, for those of us who won't remember whether "A" or "N" should be first

LGTM
once the failing tests are happy and @edsantiago 's suggestions are considered.

> once the failing tests are happy

They're failing on purpose...I'm testing the magic-string conditionals :smiley: Sorry if that wasn't clear.
[Example test failure](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5597245303685120) when PR is not set as a **draft**.

Ugh...[this is going to annoy people](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6003168971063296?logs=setup#L129) - Cirrus takes the original draft-mode state, not the current state.  So:

1. Developer pushes new PR, adds magic string to title
2. Developer goes to lunch anticipating 1.5 hours of testing
3. Instead, tests fail w/in 5-minutes because it's not draft-mode
4. Developer-on-lunch notices, and quickly switches to draft-mode and presses re-run.
5. Test fail again because the PR *was not previously* in draft-mode.
6. Annoyed-developer makes small commit change, and re-pushes.
7. Finally the developer can really go to lunch as the tests run.

I'l add a note to the docs about this.  I don't think there's much else I can do about it :cry:

Examples (success and failure) are looking good. Thanks for testing.

The repush thing is a known feature of Cirrus: it gets a snapshot as of job-start time. That's why we can't just say "add NO NEW TESTS NEEDED to the github description and try again".
okay, I think (finally) this is ready.  Rebasing and pushing one last (required) time.
